text: 10 winners and losers from the Patriots’ Day 3 picks in the NFL Draft - Pats Pulpit
fullText: New England selected six players on the final day of the NFL Draft.

text: Trump unhurt and suspect in custody after shots fired at White House correspondents' dinner - BBC
fullText: The US president is rushed out of the event at a Washington hotel, as police say the man, 31, had multiple weapons and was a "lone actor".

text: Red Sox fire manager Alex Cora after abysmal start to season - The Boston Globe
fullText: Triple-A manager Chad Tracy will serve as interim. Bench coach Ramón Vázquez, hitting coach Pete Fatse, third-base coach Kyle Hudson, and assistant hitting coach Dillon Lawson were also fired, along with hitting strategist Joe Cronin.

text: Horoscope for Sunday, 04/26/26 by Christopher Renstrom - SFGATE
fullText: LEO (July 22 - Aug. 21): You're only as good as the company you keep. Try hanging out with a livelier crowd and you'll see your spirits pick up considerably.

text: Timberwolves lose Anthony Edwards, Donte DiVincenzo to leg injuries in Game 4 - NBA
fullText: DiVincenzo’s season is over with an Achilles tendon injury and the severity of Edwards’ left knee injury remains unclear.

text: 2026 NFL Draft: Final snap grades for every team - NFL.com
fullText: Now that all seven rounds of the 2026 NFL Draft have been completed, Chad Reuter reveals his final snap grades for each team in the league.

text: 'Joe Turner's Come And Gone' Broadway Review: Taraji P. Henson Lights Up Revival - Deadline
fullText: Deadline's review of Broadway's 'Joe Turner's Come And Gone' starring Taraji P. Henson and Cedric The Entertainer.

text: 40 years after Chernobyl: Pripyat today - DW.com
fullText: After the catastrophic accident in the nearby nuclear reactor, the city of Pripyat had to be completely evacuated. Some 50,000 people left their homes forever. DW visited the town with a former resident 40 years later.

text: Trump tariff refunds put businesses on the spot - Axios
fullText: The Trump administration moved quickly to build a tariff refund system. The president warns companies not to use it.

text: 1 police officer killed, another wounded, in shooting at Chicago's Endeavor Health Swedish Hospital, officials say - ABC7 Los Angeles
fullText: A Chicago police officer was killed and another was in critical condition after a suspect opened fire while in custody inside a hospital, officials said.

text: ‘Michael’ on Pace to Break Record for Highest-Grossing Opening Week for Musical Biopic - Rolling Stone
fullText: Despite the controversy surrounding its release, 'Michael' is on pace to break the box office record for a musical biopic in its opening week.

text: Trump defends crypto legislation at private event featuring boxer Mike Tyson, Tether CEO - CoinDesk
fullText: President Donald Trump, at a Mar-a-Lago gathering of investors in his self-branded memecoin, said crypto is mainstream and banks should back off the industry's bill.

text: Latest ceasefire talks fail as Iran's top diplomat leaves Pakistan and Trump tells envoys not to go - PBS
fullText: The latest ceasefire talks between the U.S. and Iran appeared to have failed Saturday after Tehran’s top diplomat left Pakistan and President Trump said he told envoys not to go.

text: An influx of used EVs could drive down prices - The Verge
fullText: Over a million used electric cars could hit the market in the next three years driving down prices.

text: ICE re-arrests El Gamal family after judges freed them - The Texas Tribune
fullText: A federal judge Saturday afternoon ruled that an Egyptian family of six should not immediately be deported after immigration agents suddenly re-arrested the family hours earlier.

text: These UC Berkeley Students Are Leading the Fight Against Phones - KQED
fullText: They’re deleting their social media accounts and reducing their screen time. And they want you to join them.

text: For the First Time, Scientists Mapped Magnetic Fields on the Far Side of the Sun Without Seeing It - The Daily Galaxy
fullText: A part of the Sun we never see is starting to give up its secrets. Using an unexpected approach, scientists are uncovering new patterns hidden deep within familiar data.
